creator: cpghost@cordula.ws
text:
Same here with 7.0-PRERELEASE Sat Jan 26 2008 i386
while trying databases/py-sqlite3. Trying this from
Python 2.5 yields the same error:

  import sqlite3
  s = sqlite3.connect(':memory:')

It doesn't matter if databases/sqlite3 was compiled
with THREADS support or without.

Woraround: disable FTS2 during "make config" of
/databases/sqlite3, recompile and reinstall.
